methylTracer 0.99.8
  • Get started
  • Reference
  • Changelog

Aggregate DMR-level methylation per cell (in-memory) and build SingleCellExperiment

Source: R/dmrs_to_sce.R
dmrs_to_sce_memory.Rd

This function computes, for each DMR and each cell, the mean methylation signal across all windows (markers) overlapping that DMR, by first loading all relevant markers into memory as a dense matrix. This is typically much faster than repeatedly reading small slices from an HDF5-backed matrix, at the cost of higher memory usage. The result is returned as a SingleCellExperiment object with DMRs as rows and cells as columns.

dmrs_to_sce_memory(
  met,
  dmrs,
  assay_name = "meth",
  group_col = NULL,
  scale_factor = NULL,
  verbose = TRUE
)

Arguments

met

A methylTracer object containing the per-window methylation matrix X. Rows correspond to genomic windows (markers), columns to cells, and the matrix is stored in an HDF5-backed file.

dmrs

A GRanges object with DMR coordinates.

assay_name

Character; name of the assay to store DMR-level methylation values. Default "meth".

group_col

Optional character. Name of the column in the HDF5 /obs group that stores group/cluster labels for cells (e.g. "Group", "cluster", "leiden"). If provided, this is added to colData.

scale_factor

Numeric or NULL. use the default NULL (no rescaling).

verbose

Logical; whether to print progress messages. Default TRUE.

Value

A SingleCellExperiment object with:

  • rows = DMRs (rowRanges set to dmrs);

  • columns = cells (from met@sample_name);

  • assay assay_name = matrix of DMR × cell mean methylation.

Details

Marker coordinates are inferred from met@marker_name, assuming they are of the form "chr_start_end". Overlaps between markers and DMRs are computed with findOverlaps(), and only markers that overlap at least one DMR are loaded into memory. For each DMR, the mean methylation per cell is computed as the mean over all overlapping markers, ignoring NA values.

Contents

Developed by Zetian Jia, Xiang Li.

Site built with pkgdown 2.2.0.
